signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry + Options
signals.text: USD/JPY at 158.95 — 300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid despite a 7% tumble in WTI oil following US-Iran diplomacy headlines. JPY COT LF net short -81,624 (crowded carry) remains vulnerable to safe-haven shocks, but 25d RR at -1.41 confirms the options market is already paying for downside protection. Carry dominates while VIX remains at 16.6 (normal volatility), but intervention risk rises on any retest of 160.00.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -15,435 (specs fading hawkishness) vs RBNZ hike bias priced for the 27 May meeting. With NZD 1W avg performance at -0.600% (G8 laggard), a hawkish delivery or signal from the RBNZ introduces significant short-squeeze risk. NZD/USD at 0.5852 faces a crowded spec short unwind if the 2.25% OCR is hiked or forward guidance shifts higher.

signals.title: CHF/NZD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Carry Divergence
signals.text: CHF/NZD G8 spread at 0.929% as CHF outperforms (+0.329%) while NZD lags (-0.600%). CHF strength persists despite a -225bp carry disadvantage vs NZD, signaling a shift toward defensive positioning as US-Iran headlines impact energy markets. NZD real rate of -0.83% (most accommodative in G8) further weighs on the laggard. Watch for CHF/NZD extension if the Strait of Hormuz diplomacy stalls.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Volatility + Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7166 — IV at 14.1% vs HV30 at 8.3% generates a +5.8pt VRP, the second highest in the complex, signaling heavy institutional hedging. This aligns with a massive divergence: COT LF net long +57,180 (74.5% long) vs retail 76% short. Institutional conviction is high, but the extreme retail fade and high vol premium suggest a volatile breakout attempt toward 0.7200. Confirmation required via RBA meeting on 16 Jun.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Volatility Premium + CESI
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.7835 — IV 13.9% vs HV30 6.1% creates an extreme +7.8pt VRP, pricing significant tail-risk protection. While USD CESI at +24 (strong positive) supports the greenback, the 375bp carry advantage is being challenged by CHF safe-haven demand. Negative 252d correlation between DXY and SPX (z=+1.78σ) suggests USD is currently trading as a funding currency rather than a haven. Watch for VRP compression to signal a return to carry-trade dominance.
